title: quercus petraea subf. normalis
intro: Quercus petraea subf. normalis, also known as sessile oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree that belongs to the white oak group. It has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus petraea subf. normalis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, tonics, and salves for topical application.
